title: ranunculus transversalis
intro: Ranunculus transversalis, also known as Butcher's Broom or Ruscus aculeatus, is a perennial plant species in the family Ranunculaceae, typically found in woodland areas and hedgerows. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Ranunculus transversalis has been found to have health benefits such as improving circulation, reducing swelling, and alleviating varicose veins and haemorrhoids. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tea, tincture, and cream, and is often used topically to promote healthy blood flow and relieve pain.
